Your dedications and song requests from the 1950s-90s. Can you guess the Stuck in the Middle song? Text 81012, email moneyfornothing@bbc.co.uk or call 03700 100 110.
Film-maker Jonny Owen chats to Lucy about his new series, Britain's Murder Map, and Saffron from Republica on why they are marking 30 years of their hit song Ready to Go with a tour.
Live commentary, guests and analysis. Your home for Welsh sport.
Molly Palmer sits in for Bethan playing brilliant music, including new Welsh releases in The Wales Mix.
Adam chats to Art School Girlfriend about her new album, Lean In. We dive into the vault to uncover an interview with Slash from Guns N' Roses, plus the best in new Welsh music.

Rebecca Evans champions the spoken and written words of Richard Burton and the literary ambitions of Pontrhydyfen's favourite son.

programmes.show_name: The Swansea Boys Who Built Bombs
programmes.show_description: The little known story of the Welsh scientists who developed atomic weapons and how their research eventually turned to peace.
A Pembrokeshire dairy farm diversifies into adventure tourism, while north Wales farmers prepare for a charity challenge ahead of the Royal Welsh Show.
The Reverend Sue Phillips, from Elim Baptist Church in Pontllanfraith, leads a service that is the first in a series on the Resurrection appearances.
Vaughan Roderick with political news, discussions and analysis, plus an election leader interview.

A second dose of Owen Money for the weekend. It's nonstop music all the way, with classics from the 60s, 70s and 80s, plus your dedications!
Robin brings you the best in music and guests for your Sunday afternoon. Call 03700 100 110 Text 81012 or message us via X @bbcradiowales.
Music, entertainment, and great guests for your Sunday afternoon.
Roy chats with folk musician George Furey of The Fureys as they continue their farewell tour in Wales. And novelist C P Davies shares some of the secrets of fictional Aberafanc.
Rhys Locke, minister at Saron Chapel in Crynant, blends the nostalgia , memories and story of a community pulling together in the Welsh Valleys at Easter.

Frank Hennessy presents the best in folk, roots and acoustic music from the Celtic countries and beyond.
The finest musical melodies, exquisite performances from stage and screen, and orchestral and choral works from Wales and the world. There's no need to book your seat, just listen and relax.
